data.content: We can access three types of proxies:

items: getProxi(): Here we have access to all the proper parameters of the component and any connected external stores.
getSelfProxi(): Here we have access only to the component's states/props.
getBoundedProxi(): Here we have access only to the states/props of connected external stores.

data.content: It is important that the properties of connected stores are unique and do not cause collisions. The module is designed to maintain a reasonable level of simplicity. If you design a store to support one or more stores, the choice of property names becomes important. Compared to using tools like aliases, designing a proper naming convention allows you to manage the code in a simpler and more consistent way, both at the user level and at the library design level. The choice is debatable; at the moment I consider it the most coherent.

An additional advantage of having a unified state manager is portability. A proxy that rationally unifies all the states of a store (or of a mobJs component) allows for easier use as a parameter for utility functions.

Proxy Advantages:
  • It can be used by the computed module to automatically detect dependencies without needing to declare them manually.
  • It simplifies state management by allowing both set and get operations.
  • It allows for the efficient use of LSP (Language Server Protocol) operations when types are used.
